title: Velvety Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich, umami-packed lamb tagine with saffron and aromatic spices, finished with fresh mint for a velvety, keto-friendly romance.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a tagine or heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add lamb in batches and brown on all sides; remove and set aside.
Add sliced onion and minced garlic to the pot; cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle in cumin, paprika, cinnamon, and ginger; stir and cook for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Pour in diced tomatoes and stir to combine; cook for 3 minutes until tomatoes soften.
Return browned lamb to the pot, pour in the lamb broth, and stir.
Add the saffron infusion, bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 45 minutes.
Stir occasionally, ensuring the lamb stays moist and the sauce thickens slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Fold in chopped mint leaves; stir for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Serve hot, optionally over cauliflower rice or with a side of roasted vegetables.
Enjoy the velvety texture and aromatic flavors.
